headline: Myanmar FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — Day 2 conduct continuity after May 12 school-airstrike atrocity. May 14 confirms death toll rose to 25 (initial 22 students + 2 teachers; 2 additional students died from injuries May 14). Approximately 100 children were studying at the school at strike time; at least 100 others wounded. Child victims aged 7-16; volunteer teachers in early 20s. The May 12 Oe Htein Kwin / Depayin school airstrike is now one of the deadliest attacks on children by the military since the 2021 coup. Fortify Rights and Malaysia have publicly called for regional aviation fuel embargo on the junta. Daily airstrike pattern continues. Floor sustained; conduct continuity documented.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day 2 conduct continuity after May 12 school-airstrike atrocity. May 14 death toll update: 25 dead (initial 22 students + 2 teachers; 2 additional students from grades 2 and 3 died from injuries). ~100 children studying at school at strike time; 100+ wounded. One of the deadliest attacks on children by the military since 2021 coup. International response: Fortify Rights + Malaysia call for regional aviation fuel embargo on the junta.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: May 14 death-toll update: 25 dead (was 24 in May 12 cycle); 2 additional students from grades 2 and 3 died from injuries. Confirms severity of the May 12 school-airstrike event.

May 12 school-airstrike scale clarification: approximately 100 children were studying at school at strike time; at least 100 others wounded; child victims aged 7-16; volunteer teachers in their early 20s.

International accountability mechanism activation: Fortify Rights + Malaysia public call for ASEAN to impose regional aviation fuel embargo on Myanmar junta (multiple corroborating sources May 14-16 2025 anchor; sustained 2026 call). Aviation-fuel-supply targeting is a structural fuel-chain accountability mechanism distinct from individual incident response.
Pattern: One of the deadliest attacks on children by the military since the 2021 coup.
conduct_documentation.civilian_protected_site_pattern_continua: Three consecutive weeks of strikes on protected civilian sites — schools (May 12), hospitals (May 7 Winmana), markets (May 10 Magway carry-over) — documented across separate geographic zones (Sagaing, Magway). The 25-dead-school-airstrike is now the highest-severity discrete atrocity event in this 3-week pattern. International accountability call (aviation-fuel embargo) is the most prominent international-system response in this window.
conduct_documentation.international_legal_context_carry_over: Timor-Leste universal-jurisdiction prosecution active (per scanner record). HRW 2026 World Report documents ongoing civilian targeting. Cumulative documentation continues to strengthen any future ICC/universal-jurisdiction case.
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Three consecutive weeks of protected-civilian-site targeting (now confirmed as 25-dead school airstrike at peak severity) + activated international aviation-fuel embargo call raises floor-exit evidentiary burden substantially. Floor exit would require: (a) cessation of airstrikes on civilian-protected sites; (b) accountability process for documented atrocities including the May 12 school airstrike; (c) engagement with international aviation-fuel embargo demand. None in trajectory.

key_evidence: May 12 2026 (carry-over from May 12 cycle): Junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Oe Htein Kwin / Depayin village, Sagaing Region — initial 22 students + 2 teachers killed (Moemaka, RFA, France24).

May 14 2026 (Day-2 update, in-window): Death toll rose to 25 — 2 additional students from grades 2 and 3 died from injuries sustained in the attack. Approximately 100 children were studying at the school at strike time; at least 100 others wounded (The Diplomat).
May 12 2026 victim demographics: child victims aged 7-16; two volunteer teachers in their early 20s killed.
School run by the opposition National Unity Government (NUG) in Depayin, Sagaing Region, ~160km north of Mandalay.
May 7 2026 (anchor): Hospital airstrike in Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing — civilian-protected medical site (carry-over).
International accountability activation: Fortify Rights + Malaysia call for ASEAN to impose regional aviation fuel embargo on Myanmar junta.
Pattern: One of the deadliest attacks on children by the military since the February 2021 coup.
HRW 2026 World Report Myanmar chapter documents ongoing civilian targeting pattern.

evidence.source: The Diplomat — Death Toll From Air Strike on Myanmar School Rises to 25
evidence.finding: Death toll update to 25 — 2 additional students from grades 2 and 3 died from injuries. ~100 children studying at strike time; 100+ wounded; child victims aged 7-16; volunteer teachers in early 20s.

evidence.source: Radio Free Asia — Junta bombs a school in central Myanmar, kills at least 20 students
evidence.finding: Initial RFA report May 12 — 20+ students killed; opposition-run school in Sagaing Region.

evidence.source: France24 — Children are innocent: Myanmar families in grief after school air strike
evidence.finding: Family testimony documentation; community-level grief evidence.

evidence.source: Fortify Rights — Malaysia: Impose Regional Aviation Fuel Embargo on Myanmar Junta
evidence.finding: International accountability mechanism activation: aviation-fuel-supply targeting as structural fuel-chain accountability mechanism.

evidence.source: Frontier Myanmar — Regime airstrike on school leaves trauma in its wake
evidence.finding: Independent Myanmar-domestic media documentation of community trauma; civilian-target framing.

evidence.source: Moemaka — Daily casualties amid ongoing airstrikes
evidence.finding: Daily airstrike pattern continues across Sagaing, Chin State, Rakhine State, Karen State (carry-over).
Anchor: ongoing civilian targeting pattern documented.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. Day-2 conduct continuity and death-toll update documented.

negativeSignals: May 12 Oe Htein Kwin / Depayin school airstrike — death toll now 25 (Day-2 update); ~100 children at school during strike; 100+ wounded; child victims aged 7-16; one of deadliest attacks on children since 2021 coup; three-week protected-civilian-site targeting pattern (schools, hospitals, markets); daily airstrike pattern continues; Fortify Rights/Malaysia aviation-fuel embargo call.
positiveSignals: None internal to the state. External: international accountability mechanism activation (aviation-fuel embargo call; Timor-Leste universal-jurisdiction prosecution) documents accountability-mechanism strengthening, but does not reflect Myanmar junta compassion behavior.
sector_context: Countries floor-cluster. Myanmar distinguished this cycle by Day-2 confirmation that May 12 school airstrike is the highest-severity discrete child-targeted atrocity in active rotation. Cross-cluster: civilian-protected-site systematic targeting category compounds across schools/hospitals/markets — relevant for any conflict-zone floor entity.
open_actions_resolved: May 12 school-airstrike Day-2 death-toll update documented (25 dead)
International aviation-fuel embargo call documented as new external-accountability category
open_actions_carried: Watch: junta response to international aviation-fuel embargo call
Watch: any cessation of strikes on civilian-protected sites
Watch: any further death-toll updates as injured remain in critical condition
Floor exit criteria require: cessation of airstrikes on civilian-protected sites + accountability process for May 12 school airstrike + ASEAN aviation-fuel embargo engagement — none in trajectory
